content.text: In PlayerProfile, I want the player name to take up the middle of the row it's on, with the previous and next arrows taking up as little of that row as possible and the two arrows at the same height position in the container. The player name should be next to the year drop-down and all should fit in one row until the viewport gets too narrow. Avoid CSS and use Mantine styling wherever possible.

The current layout uses `span="content"` for all three columns (arrows + title), which doesn't give the title the "middle" of the row — it just sits between the arrows with only their combined width consumed. The arrows also have mismatched `align` values (`flex-start` vs `flex-end`), which is why they're not vertically aligned.

I'll fix this by making the title column `span="auto"` (so it fills the remaining row width), centering its content with Mantine's `Center`, and aligning all three columns to `center` so the arrows sit at the same vertical position regardless of whether the title/dropdown wraps.

content.text:
# Run the project's mandatory finalisation checklist before reporting any task as done

## Steps

1. Run `npm run typecheck` and capture all output.
2. Run `npm run lint` and capture all output.
3. Run `npx knip` and capture all output.
4. If any of the three produced errors or warnings:
   - For lint issues — run `npm run lint:fix` first to auto-fix what it can, then re-run `npm run lint` to see what remains.
   - For knip findings — remove the unused files, exports, or dependencies it reports.
   - Fix **every** remaining issue — do not skip, suppress, or dismiss any output.
   - If you are unsure whether a problem existed before your changes, run `git stash`, repeat all three commands to establish a pre-change baseline, then `git stash pop` and fix only the new issues.
   - After fixing, re-run all three commands to confirm they are clean.
5. Repeat until all three commands exit with code 0 and report no errors or warnings.

## Done criterion

`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npx knip` all exit with code 0 and report no errors or warnings. Only then report the task as complete.

## Notes

- Fix issues in the order they appear — later errors are often caused by earlier ones.
- Avoid silencing a lint rule or adding a `@ts-ignore` / `eslint-disable` comment to make the output go away — fix the underlying problem. If a suppression is truly necessary (false positive, required vendor API), scope it as narrowly as possible (single line, specific rule) and add a short inline comment explaining why.
- If a typecheck error is in a generated file (`prisma/generated/`, `prisma/zod/`), do not edit it — regenerate it with `npx prisma generate` instead.

content.text:
All three checks pass clean with no errors or warnings.

## Summary

Fixed the `PlayerProfile` header row ([PlayerProfile.tsx:66-90](src/components/PlayerProfile/PlayerProfile.tsx#L66-L90)):

- Changed the title column from `span="content"` to `span="auto"` so it fills the remaining row width (its middle) instead of just sitting between the arrows.
- Wrapped `TitleWithYearDropdown` in Mantine's `Center` to horizontally center the name+dropdown within that space.
- Set `align="center"` on the outer `Grid` and all three `GridCol`s (previously `flex-start`/`center`/`flex-end`), so the two arrows now sit at the same vertical position regardless of whether the title/dropdown wraps to two lines.

No raw CSS was introduced — all changes use Mantine's `Grid`/`GridCol`/`Center` props. Typecheck, lint, and knip all pass clean, and the existing `PlayerProfile` tests still pass.

Similarly, without adding CSS unless strictly necessary, I think SimpleGrid is the wrong choice for inside TitleWithYearDropdown - all I want is the two components to be centred in their container, with the text baselines at the same height, centred overall left to right with a natural gap between them (basically so the drop-down looks like a natural continuation of the header text) until the container is too narrow to hold them, at which point they should flow onto two rows.

content.text:
All clean — typecheck, lint, and knip exit with no errors or warnings, and the component's tests pass.

## Summary

Replaced `SimpleGrid` with `Group` in [TitleWithYearDropdown.tsx](src/components/TitleWithYearDropdown/TitleWithYearDropdown.tsx#L36):

- `SimpleGrid` forced the title and year-dropdown into equal-width columns (each taking 50% of the container), which is why the gap between them wasn't natural and they weren't truly centred as a unit.
- `Group justify="center" align="baseline" gap="xs" wrap="wrap"` sizes each child to its natural content width, centres the pair as a whole, aligns their text baselines, and — since it's just flexbox `wrap`, not a breakpoint — flows the dropdown onto its own row automatically whenever the container gets too narrow to hold both, with no explicit breakpoints needed.
